children.description: {{Speciesbox | image = Coral (Lobophyllia corymbosa), mar Rojo, Egipto, 2023-04-18, DD 165.jpg | image_caption = Lobophyllia corymbosa | status_system = IUCN3.1 | status = LC | status_ref = | taxon = Lobophyllia corymbosa | authority = (Forskål), 1775) | synonyms = * Lobophyllia eydouxi Milne Edwards & Haime, 1849 * Lobophyllia fistulosa Milne Edwards & Haime, 1849 * Lobophyllia ringens Milne Edwards & Haime, 1849 * Lobophyllia rudis Milne Edwards & Haime, 1849 * Madrepora corymbosa Forskål, 1775 * Mussa cactus Dana, 1846 * Mussa corymbosa (Forskål, 1775) * Mussa eydouxi (Milne Edwards & Haime, 1849) * Mussa fistulosa (Milne Edwards & Haime, 1849) * Mussa glomerata (Milne Edwards & Haime, 1849) * Mussa ringens (Milne Edwards & Haime, 1849) * Mussa rudis (Milne Edwards & Haime, 1849) }} thumb|Colony in the Red Sea Lobophyllia corymbosa, also known as lobed cactus coral or brain root coral, is a species of large polyp stony coral in the family Lobophylliidae.
